headlines.title: House Votes to Restrain Trump’s War Powers
headlines.description: There were cheers in the House of Representatives after four Republicans broke from President Donald Trump, joining Democrats to pass a preliminary resolution to stop the war on Iran. The president said a deal with Iran could come soon and that the near-daily exchanges of fire between the United States and Iran and attacks on U.S. allies on the Gulf, are normal. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump to Tap Acting AG Todd Blanche to Lead DOJ Permanently
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is making changes at the Justice Department, nominating acting attorney general Todd Blanche as its permanent director. Blanche replaced Pam Bondi after her firing and since then has notably investigated several of Trump’s perceived enemies.

headlines.title: Marjane Satrapi, French-Iranian author of ‘Persepolis,’ dies at 56
headlines.description: Marjane Satrapi, the French-Iranian author and illustrator behind the popular graphic novel series and film “Persopolis,” has died, the French presidency said Thursday.

headlines.title: The Knicks’ Jalen Brunson can already do what the Spurs are still figuring out
headlines.description: When the New York Knicks needed a big basket against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the NBA Finals, they turned to Jalen Brunson. He delivered.

headlines.title: Scott Pelley Speaks out After His Termination From ’60 Minutes’
headlines.description: Scott Pelley is accusing CBS News editor-in-chief Bari Weiss of not telling the truth about his termination from “60 Minutes,” disputing her claim that leadership attempted to find “a way back” with him. NBC’s Erin McLaughlin reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: DHS watchdog finds use-of-force issues and safety and sanitation concerns at Louisiana ICE center
headlines.description: A DHS watchdog report revealed that staff members at Winn Correctional Center used a prohibited chokehold to “gain control” of a person being held there and stabbed another in the hand with a pen when an officer could not close the door to a housing unit.

headlines.title: New video shows apparent Ukrainian attacks on Russian targets in St. Petersburg
headlines.description: New video shows apparent Ukrainian attacks on Russian targets in St. Petersburg during a major economic forum. NBC News' Keir Simmons reports.

headlines.title: Three boys were found guilty of rape at knifepoint. A U.K. judge let them walk.
headlines.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the case would go to the Court of Appeal to be reviewed — but advocates say the initial sentences speak to a wider failing.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: New York Democrats take first step toward drawing new congressional lines ahead of 2028
headlines.description: Years after handing redistricting power over to an independent commission, New York Democrats want it back as they join the national map-drawing battle with an eye on the 2028 election

headlines.title: Terry Rozier must forfeit most of his $26 million NBA salary, arbitrator rules
headlines.description: The arbitrator decided last month that Rozier, who has pleaded not guilty in a sports gambling investigation, breached his obligations to play for the Miami Heat.

headlines.title: Laos cave survivor recounts days trapped in dark, flooded cave
headlines.description: Through the long days and nights he and four other men were trapped inside a flooded cave, all Mued Duangsomdy could think about was food.
